generated_comment:
A: Arre yaar, these days boys get offended by every word. In the fauj also people used those mother-sister type gaalis casually, it was never about any actual woman.
B: But it is still abusive, no?
A: Haan, abusive hai, but don't overthink it. Sometimes a fellow is behaving weak and you just tell him not to act like a woman and move on.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-body/chastity-based abuse as harmless expression and explicitly uses feminizing comparison ('act like a woman') as an insult. It matches the frame's surface manifestations of defending gaalis as 'just words' and treating femininity as degrading.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 52. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your son just told you he wants to sign a prenup before his wedding next year. His fiance is upset about it. You are sitting with your son in the study and telling him whether you think he is right to want a prenup and what you think the fiance's reaction says about her intentions.
